subject: Chemistry
title: Helium: Found on the Sun First
description: A 60-second lesson on Helium: Found on the Sun First.

hook.text: Helium was discovered on the Sun 27 years before anyone found it on Earth.

buildup.text: During an 1868 solar eclipse, astronomers saw an unknown yellow line in the Sun's spectrum.

discovery.text: They named the mystery element helium, from 'helios'—Greek for Sun. Nobody had ever seen it on Earth.

twist.text: In 1895, William Ramsay found helium trapped in uranium ore. The Sun's element was here all along.

climax.text: Helium is the second most abundant element in the universe—but rare on Earth because it escapes to space.

punchline.text: Found on the Sun first. Rare on Earth because it floats away.

quiz.question: How was helium first discovered?
options: By observing an unknown spectral line during a solar eclipse
By cooling air to extremely low temperatures
